passages.text: The rapid expansion of urban areas has led to significant environmental changes. Scientists have (16) ---- that the loss of green spaces contributes to the 'heat island' effect. While some cities (17) ---- sustainable policies, others continue to prioritize industrial growth (18) ---- ecological preservation. This trend is particularly (19) ---- in developing nations where economic pressures are high. Consequently, urban planners are now (20) ---- for more integrated approaches to city design.

The concept of 'Deep Time' was first introduced by James Hutton in the 18th century, revolutionizing our understanding of the Earth's history. Before this breakthrough, most people believed the Earth was only a few thousand years old, based on literal interpretations of religious texts. Hutton argued that the geological processes we see today, such as erosion and sedimentation, operate at such slow rates that they would require millions of years to shape the landscape as we see it. This perspective shifted the focus from sudden catastrophes to gradualism, laying the foundation for modern geology and evolutionary biology.

The Anthropocene is a proposed geological epoch dating from the commencement of significant human impact on Earth's geology and ecosystems. Though the exact start date remains a matter of academic debate, many researchers point to the Industrial Revolution in the late 18th century as the definitive catalyst. During this period, the widespread use of fossil fuels and the mechanization of labor began to alter the chemical composition of the atmosphere. Carbon dioxide levels, which had remained relatively stable for millennia, began a precipitous rise. Furthermore, the massive scale of human-led land transformation, including deforestation and urbanization, has resulted in what some biologists term the sixth mass extinction event. Critics of the term Anthropocene argue that it oversimplifies the diverse impacts of different human societies, yet the consensus remains that the scale of anthropogenic change is unprecedented in the history of the planet.

Quantum entanglement is a physical phenomenon that occurs when a pair or group of particles is generated, interact, or share spatial proximity in a way such that the quantum state of each particle cannot be described independently of the state of the others. This remains true even when the particles are separated by a large distance. Measurements of physical properties such as position, momentum, spin, and polarization performed on entangled particles can be found to be perfectly correlated. For instance, if a pair of entangled particles is generated such that their total spin is known to be zero, and one particle is found to have clockwise spin on a first axis, then the spin of the other particle, measured on the same axis, will be found to be counterclockwise. This led Albert Einstein to derisively refer to the concept as 'spooky action at a distance,' as it seemed to violate the local realism of classical physics.

The Renaissance was not merely a rebirth of classical learning but a fundamental shift in the human perspective of the self and the world. Central to this movement was Humanism, an intellectual movement that emphasized the value and agency of human beings, individually and collectively. Humanists preferred critical thinking and evidence over dogma or superstition. This shift was fueled by the rediscovery of Greek and Roman texts, which had been preserved in the Byzantine Empire and the Islamic world. The invention of the printing press by Johannes Gutenberg around 1440 played a pivotal role, as it allowed for the rapid dissemination of these ideas. Consequently, literacy rates climbed, and the monopoly of the Church over knowledge began to erode, paving the way for the Scientific Revolution and the Enlightenment.

questions.question:
Mark: Have you noticed how many companies are moving toward remote-first models even after the pandemic?
Sarah: I have. It seems they've realized that productivity doesn't necessarily drop when people work from home.
Mark: --- 
Sarah: That's true, but for many, the trade-off is worth it for the flexibility they gain.

options.A: I think everyone should be forced to go back to the office immediately.
options.B: Indeed, though some managers still struggle with not being able to oversee staff in person.
options.C: Remote work is actually much more expensive for the employees.
options.D: I've never liked working from home; it's too distracting.
options.E: Why do you think remote work is becoming so unpopular these days?

questions.question:
Professor: The results of your lab report are quite impressive, but you need to clarify your methodology section.
Student: Thank you, Professor. I was worried that I hadn't explained the variable control clearly enough.
Professor: --- 
Student: I'll get on that right away and submit the revised version by Friday.

options.A: You don't need to change anything; it's perfect as it is.
options.B: The variables were the only part that actually made sense.
options.C: Precisely. If you provide more detail there, the whole paper will be much stronger.
options.D: I think you should change the entire topic of your research.
options.E: Wait, did you actually conduct this experiment yourself?

questions.question:
Tourist: Excuse me, is this the right bus for the National Museum?
Local: No, this one goes in the opposite direction. You need the 42B on the other side of the street.
Tourist: --- 
Local: About every ten minutes. You won't have to wait long.

options.A: Is the museum open on Mondays?
options.B: How much does a ticket cost?
options.C: Thank you. Do you know how often that bus runs?
options.D: Can I walk there from here instead?
options.E: Which museum did you say it was?

questions.question:
Clara: I'm really struggling to finish this project by the deadline.
Jason: Would you like me to take over the data entry part for you?
Clara: --- 
Jason: Don't mention it. We're a team, after all.

options.A: No, I prefer to do everything by myself.
options.B: That would be a huge lifesaver, Jason! Are you sure?
options.C: I don't think you're qualified to help me.
options.D: Why are you always so busy?
options.E: The deadline was actually moved to next month.

questions.question:
Interviewer: Where do you see yourself in five years within this company?
Candidate: I hope to have mastered the technical aspects of this role and be leading a creative team.
Interviewer: --- 
Candidate: I believe my previous experience in project management has prepared me well for that.

options.A: That sounds ambitious. What makes you think you're ready for leadership?
options.B: We don't actually have any leadership positions here.
options.C: I think five years is too long to stay in one company.
options.D: Can you tell me more about your hobbies?
options.E: Are you planning to leave the company before then?

questions.question: One of your friends has been feeling very stressed lately due to her heavy workload. You want to offer her some practical help rather than just giving her advice. What do you say?

options.A: You really should learn how to manage your time better.
options.B: Why don't you just quit your job if it's so stressful?
options.C: I have some free time this afternoon; is there any specific task I can help you with?
options.D: I'm also very busy, so I know exactly how you feel.
options.E: Have you tried meditating? It's supposed to be great for stress.

questions.question: You are at a restaurant and the waiter brings you a dish that you didn't order. You want to point this out politely and ask for the correct dish. What do you say?

options.A: This looks terrible. Take it back immediately and bring me what I asked for.
options.B: Excuse me, I think there might be a mistake. I ordered the grilled salmon, but this is the pasta.
options.C: I'll just eat this, but I'm not going to pay for it.
options.D: Why does it take so long to get a simple order right in this place?
options.E: Is this what everyone else is eating today?

options.A: It’s okay, university isn't for everyone. You can just work at the cafe.

options.A: I've deleted your file, but it's your fault for not having a backup.

options.C: I am so sorry, I've accidentally deleted your file. Let's call IT together and see if we can get it back.
options.D: You can just rewrite the report; it shouldn't take you that long.
options.E: Don't worry, that file wasn't even that important anyway.

questions.question: You are in a meeting and a colleague makes a suggestion that you think is very risky for the company's budget. You want to express your concern without sounding dismissive. What do you say?

options.A: That is the worst idea I have ever heard in this office.
options.B: I see your point, but I'm concerned about how this might affect our budget. Should we look at the costs again?
options.C: We are definitely not doing that. It's too expensive.
options.D: I don't care about the budget; let's just do it and see what happens.
options.E: Could you please stop talking and let someone else speak?

questions.question: Hardly had the government announced the new tax policy when citizens began to protest in the streets.

options.A: The citizens protested so that the government would announce a new tax policy.
options.B: The government announced the tax policy because the citizens were protesting.
options.C: Immediately after the new tax policy was made public, people started protesting.
options.D: The government waited for the protests to end before announcing the new taxes.
options.E: Citizens had been protesting for a long time before the tax policy was announced.

questions.question: The manager would have promoted him if he hadn't been consistently late for work.

options.A: The manager promoted him despite his constant lateness.
options.B: He was promoted because he started coming to work on time.
options.C: His habitual lateness prevented him from getting a promotion.
options.D: Even if he had been on time, the manager wouldn't have promoted him.
options.E: He is late for work because he didn't get the promotion he wanted.

questions.question: Despite the lack of evidence, the jury found the defendant guilty of the crime.

options.A: The jury found the defendant guilty because there was a lot of evidence.
options.B: The defendant was acquitted because there wasn't enough evidence.
options.C: Although there wasn't enough proof, the jury still decided the defendant was guilty.
options.D: The jury could not reach a verdict because the evidence was missing.
options.E: The lack of evidence led the jury to believe the defendant was innocent.

questions.question: The earlier we leave for the airport, the less likely we are to miss our flight.

options.A: We will miss our flight if we leave for the airport too early.
options.B: Leaving early for the airport reduces the chance of missing the flight.
options.C: We have already missed our flight because we didn't leave early.
options.D: It doesn't matter what time we leave; we will probably miss the flight.
options.E: We should leave late so that we don't have to wait at the airport.

questions.question: It is not until you travel abroad that you truly appreciate your own culture.

options.A: You can only appreciate your own culture once you have visited other countries.
options.B: Traveling abroad makes you dislike your own culture.
options.C: You should appreciate your culture before you decide to travel abroad.
options.D: People who never travel abroad appreciate their culture the most.
options.E: Appreciating your own culture is a prerequisite for traveling to other places.

questions.question: Artificial Intelligence is rapidly transforming the healthcare industry. Algorithms can now analyze medical images with a precision that often surpasses human radiologists. ---. For instance, AI can help predict patient outbreaks or identify the most effective treatment plans based on a patient's genetic profile. However, the integration of AI also raises ethical concerns regarding data privacy.

options.A: Furthermore, its applications extend far beyond just imaging.
options.B: Therefore, many doctors are losing their jobs to machines.
options.C: In contrast, traditional medicine is still more reliable.
options.D: AI was first developed in the mid-20th century.
options.E: Patients are generally afraid of new technologies.

questions.question: Sustainable architecture aims to minimize the negative environmental impact of buildings. This is achieved through efficiency and moderation in the use of materials, energy, and development space. ---. For example, using solar panels and rainwater harvesting systems are common practices in green building design. Ultimately, the goal is to create structures that are in harmony with the natural world.

options.A: Construction costs are the primary concern for most modern architects.
options.B: Many buildings in the past were built without any regard for the environment.
options.C: The use of renewable energy sources is a key component of this approach.
options.D: Urban sprawl is one of the biggest problems facing cities today.
options.E: Architects must also consider the aesthetic appeal of their designs.

questions.question: The Great Depression of the 1930s was a severe worldwide economic downturn. It began after the stock market crash in the United States in October 1929. ---. Unemployment rates soared, and international trade plunged by more than 50%. It took the mobilization of World War II to finally pull most nations out of the economic slump.

options.A: The stock market is a very risky place to invest your money.
options.B: The effects were devastating for both rich and poor countries.
options.C: Many people enjoy studying history because it teaches us about the present.
options.D: The 1920s were known as the Roaring Twenties because of the economic boom.
options.E: Agricultural production actually increased during this period.

questions.question: Language is not just a tool for communication; it is a reflection of a culture's identity. The idioms and metaphors used in a language often reveal the values and history of its speakers. ---. When a language dies, a unique way of looking at the world is lost forever. This is why many linguists are working hard to document endangered languages.

options.A: Learning a second language can improve your cognitive abilities.
options.B: English has become the global lingua franca of the modern era.
options.C: Consequently, language loss is equivalent to the loss of cultural heritage.
options.D: Most people find it difficult to learn a new language as they get older.
options.E: Translating poetry is one of the hardest tasks for a linguist.

questions.question: Bees play a crucial role in our ecosystem as pollinators. They are responsible for pollinating a third of the food we eat, including many fruits and vegetables. ---. Factors such as pesticide use, habitat loss, and climate change are largely to blame. If the bee population continues to decline, it could lead to a global food crisis.

options.A: Honey has been used for its medicinal properties for centuries.
options.B: There are over 20,000 different species of bees in the world.
options.C: However, bee populations have been declining at an alarming rate.
options.D: Beekeeping is becoming a popular hobby in many urban areas.
options.E: Flowers attract bees by using bright colors and sweet scents.

questions.question: Choose the irrelevant sentence: (I) Photosynthesis is the process by which green plants transform light energy into chemical energy. (II) During this process, plants use sunlight to convert water and carbon dioxide into glucose. (III) Glucose provides the energy plants need to grow and reproduce. (IV) Some plants are known to grow better in acidic soil than in alkaline soil. (V) Oxygen is released as a byproduct of this vital biological reaction.

options.A: I
options.B: II
options.C: III
options.D: IV
options.E: V

(IV) Julius Caesar was a Roman general who played a critical role in the rise of the empire. (V) Most modern tourists prefer to visit Italy during the summer months for the weather.

questions.question: Choose the irrelevant sentence: (I) NASA’s James Webb Space Telescope is the most powerful telescope ever launched into space. (II) It was designed to look back in time to the very first galaxies formed after the Big Bang. (III) The telescope uses infrared light to see through clouds of cosmic dust. (IV) Astronomy is a subject that many students find fascinating during high school. (V) Since its launch, it has provided stunningly detailed images of the universe.

questions.question: Choose the irrelevant sentence: (I) Sleep is essential for maintaining both physical and mental health. (II) During sleep, the brain processes information and consolidates memories from the day. (III) Adults typically need between seven and nine hours of quality sleep per night. (IV) Caffeine is a stimulant that can help people stay awake during the day. (V) Chronic sleep deprivation is linked to serious conditions like heart disease and obesity.
